metrics.narrative: Zycus shared early-adopter benchmarks (4-6% tail-spend savings over 4-5 months, 70-80% reduction in negotiation time) on the 21 Oct 2025 discovery call, but these are third-party numbers, not McAfee's own business case. aitor gonzalez martin explicitly required an evidence-based ROI model built on McAfee's real POC data before any benchmark counts, and as of the 1 Jul 2026 email he confirmed none of the five initial use cases have been completed end-to-end, so the business case remains unproven on McAfee's own numbers.

champion.narrative: aitor gonzalez martin has been the consistent, technically detailed champion for over a year, running the POC's day-to-day feedback loop, coordinating reference calls, and pushing the product team on platform bugs. However, his tone has hardened: on 24 Jun and 1 Jul 2026 he flagged that zero of the five original use cases have completed end-to-end and called the supplier-response problem "a structural limitation," and separately noted the Nippon Gas reference call "wasn't as helpful as expected" because it ran an older ANA version with only ~20 events created. He remains engaged (continuing to schedule tests and calls) but his confidence appears to be eroding.

sources: Avoma: McAfee + Zycus Discovery - Agentic AI, 2025-10-17
competition.narrative: McAfee already runs zip (orchestration tool) for intake orchestration and integrates it rather than replaces it; pactem (post-sourcing contract negotiation) was raised by mazen el-haidari as a comparison point for post-sourcing contract work, a different part of the process than ANA. Most significantly, per Opportunity.AIS_Why__c, McAfee cut an initial nine-vendor field to three finalists for this specific AI/ANA evaluation, and Zycus has not identified the other two: unidentified parallel poc finalist(s) (mcafee's 3-vendor shortlist) remain a live, unmapped threat heading into the final stretch.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well articulated and consistent across calls: McAfee wants to run autonomous negotiation end-to-end (intake through award) for indirect tail spend after its SAP-to-Workday migration, and Customer_Business_Problem__c independently confirms goals around faster sourcing cycles, expanded event capacity, AI-based supplier recommendations, and autonomous negotiating. The complication, raised directly by the champion, is that most of McAfee's indirect spend is services (marketing, software renewals, people services) that need human-in-the-loop handling for intangible negotiation factors, which narrows where autonomous negotiation actually applies.

paper_process.narrative: No commercial pricing proposal has been delivered despite being promised on the 21 Oct 2025 discovery call (spend-tier pricing plus separate integration/implementation pricing for the ANA module). A mutual NDA was requested on that same call; there is no evidence in any read source that it has since been executed. No MSA, SOW, or Order Form activity appears anywhere in the record.

sources: Task thread referencing CFO meeting (Task 00TP700000pJ1LOMA0, 2026-06-11 context)
economic_buyer.narrative: A compelling event is on record (Compelling_Event__c: "CEO mandate to reduce cost and increase efficiencies"), and internal reviews have occurred at the executive level (a CFO meeting around 11 Jun 2026 and a CEO/CIO video review the week of 24 Jun 2026), but these are passive, internal McAfee reviews. Zycus has had no direct engagement with the CFO, CEO, or CIO at any point in this record. Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c and Decision_Maker_Identified__c are both false in Salesforce, consistent with the absence of direct executive contact.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: nick priest (Head of Global Procurement)
decision_process.narrative: McAfee narrowed an initial nine-vendor field to three finalists specifically for this AI/ANA evaluation (per AIS_Why__c), and the POC itself functions as the formal down-select mechanism. Internal alignment runs through aitor gonzalez martin and nick priest (Head of Global Procurement), with passive executive sign-off points (CFO, CEO/CIO) layered on top. No documented decision date or formal award process has been shared with Zycus.

sources: Avoma: McAfee + Zycus Discovery - Agentic AI, 2025-10-17
decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are visible through conversation, not a formal document: a proposed tail-spend threshold ($50k, with McAfee wanting to test up to $100k), completion of five defined use cases end-to-end, AI accuracy/hallucination tolerance, and supplier response rate all surfaced on the 17 and 21 Oct 2025 discovery calls. As of 1 Jul 2026, McAfee is still relaunching those same use cases with test suppliers, meaning the criteria exist but have not yet been evaluated against real results.

Zycus has not sent a commercial proposal or validated pricing against McAfee's budget. If the unknown POC competitor has lower pricing or Zycus's spend-based tier pricing is too high, McAfee could choose the cheaper option even if Zycus's POC results are stronger.

The 11 Jun Next_Step mentions a 'McAfee meeting w CFO on Monday' with no follow-up, and the 4 Jun note mentions a CEO/CIO review of the ANA video, but this is a passive internal review, not active Zycus engagement with the EB. With 24 days to close and no EB sign-off on the $200k investment, the deal cannot move to the relevant stakeholder or Negotiation. The champion (Aitor) can drive the POC to a positive outcome, but he cannot close the deal.

Even if the POC concludes positively in the next 1-2 weeks, the remaining gates (EB validation, pricing negotiation, MSA/Order Form, InfoSec if required, signature) cannot complete in ~2 weeks. items.detail: Unknown POC competitor creates invisible political risk. Zycus does not know if the competitor has a stronger internal advocate (e.g., an executive sponsor Zycus has not met), a preferred-vendor relationship, or a lower price that has already been validated. If the competitor has been more aggressive in mapping and engaging the economic buyer or senior stakeholders, Zycus could lose the POC even with equal or better technical results.

McAfee's majority spend is indirect services (marketing, facilities, software renewals), and the 21 Oct demo showed that complex/intangible services may require human-in-loop for non-pricing negotiations (e.g., SEO scope, hypercare weeks, contract terms). If too many of McAfee's service categories require human intervention, the autonomous-negotiation value proposition shrinks — fewer deals run fully autonomously means lower throughput gains and weaker savings, undermining the ROI justification. The buyer has not yet confirmed how many of their tail-spend categories are quotable (fully autonomous) vs. complex (human-in-loop required).

The close date has been revised roughly eight times since creation (2025-12-05 -> 2026-12-04 -> 2026-09-18 -> 2026-01-30 -> 2026-02-27 -> 2026-04-30 -> 2026-05-29 -> 2026-04-17 -> 2026-05-29 -> 2026-06-04 -> 2026-06-30 -> 2026-07-31, per OpportunityFieldHistory), most recently on 2026-06-29. With today 2026-07-07 and close_date 2026-07-31, only 24 days remain, but McAfee's own POC-completion target is the week of 2026-07-24 (per Aitor's 1 Jul 2026 email) - leaving roughly one week to clear pricing, EB sign-off, and paper after use-case validation even lands on time.

north_star_verdict.headline: Aitor remains an engaged, cooperative champion, but the core POC deliverable has stalled: zero of five use-case events have been completed end-to-end, the champion has floated a 'structural limitation' on supplier response, the Nippon Gas reference call reinforced doubt rather than removing it, and no commercial proposal has ever reached McAfee, with the buyer's own retest target (week of 24 Jul 2026) leaving only about a week before the 31 Jul close date.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Move off Best Case to Pipeline; realistic close is Q3 2026 (Sept) pending POC completion, a delivered commercial proposal, and direct CFO/EB engagement.

flags: The core value prop (supplier response rate) is under live stress-test: across the 5 POC events only a fraction of suppliers have responded (e.g., 1 of 5 on the laptop RFQ), and Aitor has floated a 'structural limitation' - winning back confidence here before week-of-24-Jul is the single highest-leverage lever.
The Nippon Gas reference call underdelivered instead of reassuring; line up a second, more mature ANA reference customer before the buyer's decision window closes.

No commercial proposal has reached McAfee 8+ months after Zycus committed to one; with pricing untested against Aitor's explicit ROI gate and only ~24 days to the current close date, get pricing in front of him now rather than after POC sign-off.
Address the champion's stated doubts about execution head-on: confirm WIP fix status and give a hard, dated commitment on the remaining use cases before raising commercial topics again.
best_practice_check.summary: The highest-leverage lever right now is closing the credibility gap the champion himself has raised (unfinished use cases, an underwhelming reference, unconfirmed fixes) before asking for any further commercial commitment; single-threading is a secondary but real concern given no direct executive contact.

customer_preference.evidence: McAfee committed real internal effort to a live, hands-on POC with Zycus (test-supplier relaunches, weekly follow-ups, an executive video review) rather than a passive evaluation, but no buyer has voiced an explicit preference for Zycus over the other finalists in any read source, and the champion's recent tone (1 Jul 2026) has turned more critical.

competitive_position.summary: The named competitive picture is thin and partly adjacent rather than head-to-head: zip is McAfee's existing intake-orchestration tool being integrated with, not replaced by, Zycus; pactem was raised only as a post-sourcing contract-negotiation comparison point. The real live threat is that McAfee cut nine vendors to three for this specific evaluation and the other two finalists have never been named.

competitors.name: zip (orchestration tool)
competitors.quote: McAfee uses zip for intake orchestration; routing decisions stay in zip, with Zycus integrating alongside it.
competitors.how_we_win: Position ANA as complementary to zip's intake layer rather than a replacement, focusing on the negotiation and award steps zip does not cover.

competitors.quote: mazen el-haidari raised pactem as a comparison point for post-sourcing contract negotiation.

competitors.how_we_win: Clarify ANA's negotiation scope versus pactem's contract-stage focus to avoid overlap confusion in the buyer's mental model.

competitors.name: unidentified parallel poc finalist(s) (mcafee's 3-vendor shortlist)
competitors.quote: McAfee tightened the shortlist from 9 vendors to 3 explicitly for the AI and ANA evaluation.
competitors.how_we_win: Use the next POC review to directly ask which other vendors are in the final three and what is differentiating them, closing the current blind spot.

competitors.name: Zip
competitors.quote: McAfee mentioned looking at orchestration tools like Zip alongside Zycus during early discovery.
competitors.how_we_win: Position ANA's autonomous negotiation depth against Zip's orchestration-only scope; no re-mention in 14 months suggests it is no longer active competition.

competitors.name: PACTEM
competitors.quote: Mazen raised Pactem as an interest in post-sourcing, contract-side negotiation, distinct from Zycus's pre-award autonomous negotiation.
competitors.how_we_win: Reframe Pactem as complementary, not competitive: ANA covers pre-award negotiation, Pactem covers post-sourcing contract admin; no overlap to defend.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A genuinely live, technically deep, 14-month POC relationship with a consistently engaged champion, sitting against an unproven business case, no direct economic-buyer engagement, an unidentified competitor in the final three, and a close date (31 Jul 2026, 22 days out) that the evidence does not support.

deal_risk.text: A commercial pricing proposal has been outstanding since 21 Oct 2025 (over 8 months), with no evidence it has been delivered as of the most recent read.

The buyer's stated number-one reference request (a Tata Play call) was never actually delivered; a Nippon Gas call was substituted instead on 18-19 Jun 2026 and the champion said it did not provide the validation he was hoping for.
unidentified parallel poc finalist(s) (mcafee's 3-vendor shortlist) represent an invisible political risk heading into the final decision.
Neither the CFO nor CEO/CIO has been directly engaged, only reviewed internally, leaving no confirmed line to the actual budget approver.

win_position.text: aitor gonzalez martin has stayed engaged for over 14 months, running a live technical POC with detailed, specific feedback exchanges as recently as 1 Jul 2026, which is a genuine depth-10 engagement signal per the enterprise motion.
Neither the CFO nor the CEO/CIO has ever been directly engaged by Zycus; both reviews on record (CFO ~11 Jun 2026, CEO/CIO week of 24 Jun 2026) were internal McAfee sessions Zycus was not part of.
McAfee narrowed nine vendors to three for this evaluation (Opportunity.AIS_Why__c) but the other two finalists remain unnamed anywhere in the record, an open competitive blind spot at this stage.
As of 1 Jul 2026 zero of the five original POC use cases have completed end-to-end, which is the champion's own stated proof bar for the business case.

deal_momentum.text: A genuine two-way buyer email landed 1 Jul 2026 (8 days ago) and a weekly POC follow-up meeting is scheduled for today, 9 Jul 2026, both real buyer-side engagement rather than one-way rep outreach.

The close date has changed 11 times since opportunity creation on 6 May 2025, most recently on 29 Jun 2026 (30 Jun → 31 Jul), and the opportunity has also been reset back to an earlier stage twice (Formal Evaluation → Initial Interest on 25 Jun 2025; Shortlisted → Qualified on 13 Feb 2026) before re-advancing.
The deal has held at Shortlisted since 3 Mar 2026, 128 days as of today against roughly an 18-day expected buyer cadence for this stage, with no commercial proposal progress in that window.

customer_commitment.text: The champion continues to proactively drive the process: relaunching use cases with test suppliers, requesting fix confirmations, and reinstating the weekly follow-up cadence himself in his 1 Jul 2026 email.

The champion's tone has hardened over the last two weeks: on 24 Jun 2026 he called the Nippon Gas reference call underwhelming (older ANA version, only ~20 events created) and flagged supplier non-response as a possible "structural limitation."

hard.ais_why:
• The products on this deal are iSaaS, Agentic AI, and ANA, and Zycus has flagged AI as Very Critical for the account. The 5/4/2026 next-step note records the McAfee ANA POC launched on 5/5. On the 5/5/2025 Avoma call, the buyer confirmed direct interest in Zycus's autonomous negotiation and spend dynamics capabilities.
• The 1/19/2026 rep note captures McAfee wanting to see whether the vendor can deliver on its Agentic AI innovation claims. The 10/21/2025 note documents Aitor pushing on integrating Zycus's autonomous negotiation with Omnia for indirect tail-spend orchestration, with detailed back-and-forth on AI accuracy, hallucination prevention, benchmark confidence, and human-in-the-loop design.
• The buyer's own POC scope requires an evidence-based business case validated through real-world use cases, plus scalability and operational viability post-POC across categories. McAfee tightened the shortlist from 9 vendors to 3 explicitly for the AI and ANA evaluation, which is enterprise-grade commitment, not curiosity.

deltas.subject: McAfee's majority spend is indirect services (marketing, facilities, software renewals), and the 21 Oct demo showed that complex/intangible services may require human-in-loop for non-pricing negotiations (e.g., SEO scope, hypercare weeks, contract terms).
